3.q: The reduced auxin accumulation and higher ABA sensitivity of the lateral roots (LR) under salt stress were observed in CdWRKY2 overexpression Arabidopsis lines.

4.q: Yeast two-hybrid assays showing the interaction between CdMYB26 and CdFBX1... Pull-down assays showed the interaction between CdMYB26 and CdFBX1.1/1.2.

5.q: CdKAT1.1 and its closest homolog CdKAT1.2 … Group II exhibited twelve pairs, including … CdKAT1.2-CdKAT1.1 … intraspecific collinearity analysis revealed … collinear gene pairs among bermudagrass genes.

6.q: CdSOS1 is a 12-transmembrane protein containing a conserved SOS2 phosphorylation site, indicating that SOS-pathway-mediated Na⁺ extrusion is a highly conserved mechanism.

7.q: CdMYB26 as a negative regulator of CdMYB5... Elevated CdMYB26 levels strengthened its repression of CdMYB5, reducing CdSOS1 expression.

8.q: CdFBX1.2 facilitated CdSOS1 expression by degrading CdMYB26... CdFBX1.2 promoted the degradation of CdMYB26 through polyubiquitination modification.

9.q: CdMYB5 acted as a positive regulator of salt tolerance by directly binding to and activating CdSOS1 expression under salt stress.

10.q: EMSA, yeast one-hybrid and dual-LUC assays revealed that they directly and specifically bind to the upstream regulatory region of CdsHSP16.970, consequently promoting its expression.

11.q: EMSA, yeast one-hybrid and dual-LUC assays revealed that they directly and specifically bind to the upstream regulatory region of CdsHSP16.970, consequently promoting its expression.

12.q: A key tolerance mechanism is potassium/sodium homeostasis, governed by Shaker K+ channels... only CdKAT1.1 was rapidly upregulated.

13.q: The Na+/H+ antiporter SOS1/NHX7 plays a vital role in maintaining ion homeostasis... silencing of CdSOS1 disrupts ion homeostasis and restrains Na+ efflux under salinity stress.
